title: Filtering with WHERE

theory.introduction: The WHERE clause is used to filter records based on specified conditions. It allows you to extract only those records that meet certain criteria.

sections.title: Basic WHERE Syntax
sections.content:
The WHERE clause comes after the FROM clause:

SELECT column1, column2
FROM table_name
WHERE condition;

sections.title: Comparison Operators
sections.content:
SQL supports the following comparison operators:

  • = - Equal to
  • <> or != - Not equal to
  • > - Greater than
  • < - Less than
  • >= - Greater than or equal to
  • <= - Less than or equal to
SELECT * FROM countries
WHERE population > 100000000;

sections.title: Logical Operators
sections.content:
Combine multiple conditions using logical operators:

  • AND - Both conditions must be true
  • OR - At least one condition must be true
  • NOT - Negates a condition
SELECT * FROM countries
WHERE continent = 'Europe' 
  AND population > 50000000;

sections.title: BETWEEN Operator
sections.content:
The BETWEEN operator selects values within a given range (inclusive):

SELECT * FROM products
WHERE price BETWEEN 50 AND 100;

This is equivalent to:

SELECT * FROM products
WHERE price >= 50 AND price <= 100;

sections.title: IN Operator
sections.content:
The IN operator allows you to specify multiple values in a WHERE clause:

SELECT * FROM countries
WHERE continent IN ('Europe', 'Asia');

This is equivalent to:

SELECT * FROM countries
WHERE continent = 'Europe' OR continent = 'Asia';

sections.title: LIKE Operator
sections.content:
The LIKE operator is used to search for a specified pattern in a column:

  • % - Represents zero or more characters
  • _ - Represents exactly one character
-- Names starting with 'A'
SELECT * FROM customers
WHERE name LIKE 'A%';

-- Names with 'o' as second character
SELECT * FROM customers
WHERE name LIKE '_o%';

examples.title: Filter by range
examples.code: SELECT * FROM products WHERE price BETWEEN 50 AND 200;

examples.title: Filter with LIKE
examples.description: Find customers whose name starts with 'J'
examples.code: SELECT * FROM customers WHERE name LIKE 'J%';

exercise.instruction: Write a query to find all products that cost more than $100. Select the name and price columns from the products table.
exercise.hint: Use SELECT to choose the columns, FROM to specify the table, and WHERE with the > operator to filter by price.
exercise.solution: SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE price > 100;

learningObjectives: Use WHERE clause to filter records
Combine conditions with AND, OR, NOT

quiz.question: What does the % wildcard represent in a LIKE pattern?
quiz.explanation: The % wildcard in LIKE patterns represents zero or more characters of any type.
